title: Tags, Versions, Releases, and Provenance · Repository Trace: Signature Trust
summary: Prepare a release candidate whose source identity, version, evidence, and published reference can be independently verified. Planned artifact: Add and verify the git signature trust increment in a signed-off release evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish commit or tag signature validity, signer identity, key trust, account verification, and policy compliance.
Record clean tree, exact commit, dependency state, build/test evidence, artifacts, and checksums for a release.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Signature Trust
steps.instruction:
theory case — Release v6.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Signature Trust contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: Bound Signature Trust
steps.instruction:
theory case — Release v2.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits.

After tracing the worked code, choose the Signature Trust application whose preconditions and limits still match the supplied case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish commit or tag signature validity, signer identity, key trust, account verification, and policy compliance.

content.text: Distinguish commit or tag signature validity, signer identity, key trust, account verification, and policy compliance. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Release v6.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits. A defensible solution applies Signature Trust, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a signed-off release evidence record.

content.code:
git status --porcelain=v1
git show --no-patch --format=fuller HEAD
git tag -a "v2.1.0" -m 'Verified release candidate' HEAD
git show --no-patch "v2.1.0^{commit}"
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A cryptographically valid signature proves the change is safe. This fails the Signature Trust contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Signature Trust evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-signature-trust-1mmg9t" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Signature Trust into observable performance evidence.

Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Defend the Signature Trust model
steps.instruction: Write the theory evidence note for Signature Trust: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Release v2.1.0 targets commit a11ce00, but the working tree has an unstaged version edit. Establish source identity, clean state, annotated tag evidence, checksum, and trust limits.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish commit or tag signature validity, signer identity, key trust, account verification, and policy compliance.
